title: Did Asuka Know About Kajis death
display_title: Did Asuka Know About Kajis death
wikitext:
[]: Did Asuka Know About Kaji's death?             Quote Post by StandardEvaFan » Sat Jun 21, 2008 11:04 pm So did she know? If she didn't know would she still be so suicidal? I remember a scene where the phone rings and Asuka ask Misato to get it because she thought it was Kaji's then Misato said it would be impossible.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Jun 21, 2008 11:10 pm She didn't know until Shinji told her. NPC FTW!

[]: Re: Did Asuka Know About Kaji's death?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jun 21, 2008 11:30 pm StandardEvaFan wrote: If she didn't know would she still be so suicidal?

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Jun 22, 2008 12:07 am I read the episode guides in the project and the chronological order of things seem to point that hearing that Kaji was gone might have been the last straw. It seems the first huge blow was getting mind raped, followed by Rei defeating the angel that beat her effortlessly* . Next was the Armisael battle during which Gendo sends out Shinji in a desperate attempt to help Rei. Asuka feels even more useless because Gendo didn't bother using Shinji to help her in her battle. Later, she hears about Kaji being gone. This is followed by her memories of her mother's suicide. She is then shown in a puddle of blood in a bathtub. I do think even without the news about Kaji, something else would have pushed her over. Probably Kaworu somehow * Oddly enough, it seems every Eva or its pilot have surpassed her in some way or another over the course of the series. I'm sure Asuka was aware of that after Rei beat Arael. The MP Evas also kick her ass.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jun 22, 2008 12:22 am Iron Rooster wrote: It seems the first huge blow was getting mind raped, followed by Rei defeating the angel that beat her effortlessly* . Next was the Armisael battle during which Gendo sends out Shinji in a desperate attempt to help Rei. Asuka feels even more useless because Gendo didn't bother using Shinji to help her in her battle. Later, she hears about Kaji being gone. This is followed by her memories of her mother's suicide.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jun 22, 2008 12:25 am During the mindrape.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Jun 22, 2008 12:30 am I'm going by the Evapedia stuff. I read it here . \/ \/ \/

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jun 22, 2008 12:31 am AuraTwilight wrote: During the mindrape.

[]:             Quote Post by Skywalker007 » Mon Jun 23, 2008 1:54 am Agree with Iron booster here, Asuka has been beated by every single EVA in the series, although, the fight with MPs wasn't a fair fight. Anyway, I like to add that her EVA has been used as a tool to make Shinji insane twice: First when Kaworu used it to enter Lilith's chamber, and Second when the MPs brutally tearing it apart.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jun 23, 2008 3:19 am Skywalker007 wrote: Agree with Iron booster here, Asuka has been beated by every single EVA in the series, although, the fight with MPs wasn't a fair fight.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Jun 23, 2008 11:00 am Sachi_13 wrote: She beat Unit-04's ass, big time.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Jun 23, 2008 4:04 pm think he was being sarcastic but idano

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jun 24, 2008 9:44 am Ornette wrote: I'm not sure if the little Asuka running to tell her mother that she got selected as an Eva pilot is really her remembering at that very moment. It could have been a narrative device.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jun 24, 2008 9:47 am Who the fuck is Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jun 24, 2008 9:52 am Zuggy wrote: Who the fuck is Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jun 24, 2008 2:16 pm V wrote: Ornette wrote: I'm not sure if the little Asuka running to tell her mother that she got selected as an Eva pilot is really her remembering at that very moment. It could have been a narrative device.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jun 24, 2008 5:10 pm "not necessarily taken as chronological events"? like what? I mean there's little to jumble around: we see Asuka's mother's funeral, then we flash further back to instances of her madness. Then we see Asuka running and her mother dying. How could this NOT be "in chronological order"? What ambiguity is there?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 24, 2008 5:18 pm I think what Ornette means is that it was a flashback to previous events before we return to the present with Asuka sitting in the tub.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jun 24, 2008 5:19 pm 3 events I'm talking about, with respect with chronology between them (not within them): 1) 2005 flashback = that entire sequence with little Asuka 2) Kitchen = Learning that Kaji is gone 3) Bathtub. Those 3 may not have occured right after each other as in: Asuka was remembering her mother's death, then she was told Kaji was dead, then she ended up in a bathtub. The narrative device is: Asuka may not be recalling this stuff at all, while in the bathtub, but the audience is being told what things lead us to the "present" (Asuka in the bathtub). The wiki article made it seem chronological. I'm not sure how much more clear I can make this.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 24, 2008 5:34 pm Ornette: Fixed the page to reflect that.

I remember a scene where the phone rings and Asuka ask Misato to get it because she thought it was Kaji's then Misato said it would be impossible.
